description: Add health monitoring endpoints to whatsapp-service

context.summary: whatsapp-service is a thin translation layer. wwebjs-api exposes /session/status/{sessionId} and /session/getSessions for session discovery. n8n exposes /healthz.

design.problem: Session names are dynamic (not always 'mysession'). Multiple services to monitor (whatsapp-service, wwebjs-api, n8n). Need unified health status for monitoring tools.
design.solution: Add health endpoints to whatsapp-service that aggregate status from all components. whatsapp-service becomes the health aggregator for the WhatsApp integration stack.

endpoints.GET /health/ready: Readiness check - returns { wwebjs: bool, n8n: bool }
endpoints.GET /health/sessions: All session statuses from wwebjs-api via /session/getSessions then /session/status/{id} for each

config.WWEBJS_API_URL: URL to wwebjs-api (e.g., http://wwebjs-api:3000 or http://localhost:3000)
config.N8N_URL: Optional URL to n8n for readiness check (e.g., http://n8n:5678)
config.HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT: Timeout in ms for upstream health checks (default: 5000)

tasks: Add HealthController with /health, /health/ready, /health/sessions routes
Add HealthService to call wwebjs-api /session/getSessions and /session/status/{id}
Add types: HealthStatus, SessionHealth, ReadinessStatus
Add config validation for WWEBJS_API_URL (required) and N8N_URL (optional)
Add timeout handling for upstream calls (configurable via HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT)
Update whatsapp-service README with health endpoint documentation
Update docs/whatsapp/02-api-reference.md with health endpoints section
verification: GET /health returns 200 { status: 'ok' } when service is running
GET /health/ready returns 200 with wwebjs: true when wwebjs-api is reachable
GET /health/sessions returns list of all sessions with their connection status
GET /health/ready returns wwebjs: false (not 500) when wwebjs-api is unreachable
Timeouts are respected - slow upstream doesn't hang the health endpoint
docs/whatsapp/ planned: add health endpoint reference and monitoring examples.

notes.session_discovery: Use GET /session/getSessions to discover all active sessions dynamically, avoiding hardcoded session names
notes.graceful_degradation: Health endpoints should return partial data on partial failure, not 500
notes.docker_compose_integration: Once implemented, docker-compose.override.yml can use: healthcheck: test: curl -f http://localhost:3000/health/ready

description: Add Keycloak OIDC auth (BFF session) to service

context.summary: Replace Basic Auth admin UI with Keycloak OIDC login. Use a BFF cookie session so the SPA never handles raw access tokens.

subtasks.description: Define Keycloak roles/claims and env vars

context.summary: We need a concrete Keycloak contract (roles, tenant claim, redirect URIs) before implementing.

tasks: Define required roles: creator_admin, tenant_admin, agent, automation_engineer, read_only
Choose tenant scoping claim name (prefer a single claim like tenant_id); document fallback if absent
Define service OIDC endpoints: /auth/login, /auth/callback, /auth/logout, /auth/me and required redirect URIs
verification: Roles list matches PRD and is finalized with exact Keycloak role names.
Tenant scoping claim name is defined and documented for all environments.
Service auth endpoints and redirect paths are enumerated without placeholders.
docs/keycloak/ planned: document realm roles, claims, and redirect URI setup.
subtasks.output: Keycloak auth contract and env var list for implementation

subtasks.description: Design auth matrix and session security

context.summary: Separate route protection: public, integration (API key), dashboard (session/PAT).

tasks: Define route groups and required auth: public (/health), integration (webhook/events/register), dashboard UI+API (/dashboard, /api/*)
Define CSRF protection strategy for cookie-session endpoints (same-site + origin checks or explicit token)
Define how roles/tenantId are extracted into a typed request auth context
verification: Auth matrix covers all existing endpoints in packages/whatsapp-service/src/index.ts for clarity.
CSRF strategy is explicit, testable, and documented for cookie-session endpoints.
Request auth context shape is defined: actorType, actorId, tenantId, roles, scopes.
docs/keycloak/ planned: add a diagram for session vs API-key protected routes.
subtasks.output: Auth matrix and session security design

subtasks.description: Implement OIDC login/callback/logout and session cookies

context.prior_output: 043.2 Auth matrix and session security design
context.summary: Implement Keycloak code flow and a server-side session; protect dashboard routes; provide /auth/me for UI bootstrapping.

tasks: Implement /auth/login to redirect to Keycloak authorization endpoint
Implement /auth/callback to exchange code for tokens, validate ID token, and establish a secure server session
Implement /auth/logout to clear session and redirect to Keycloak logout
Implement /auth/me to return { authenticated, tenantId, roles, user } for the SPA
Replace/retire requireBasicAuth gating for dashboard routes (keep behind a feature flag if needed for rollback)
verification: GET /dashboard redirects to Keycloak login when the user is unauthenticated.
After login, GET /auth/me returns roles and tenantId for the current user.
No raw access tokens are stored in browser storage (localStorage/sessionStorage).
docs/keycloak/ planned: include troubleshooting for redirect, cookie, and proxy issues.
subtasks.output: Working Keycloak login/logout with server-side session and /auth/me bootstrap endpoint

subtasks.description: Add auth tests and Keycloak setup docs

context.summary: Add test coverage and document required Keycloak realm/client configuration and environment variables.

tasks: Add unit/integration tests for auth flow endpoints (login redirect, callback error handling, /auth/me)
Document required Keycloak client config (redirect URIs, roles, tenant claim) and required env vars in docs/
Add security notes: cookie flags, CSRF strategy, and token refresh strategy (if applicable)
verification: Run `npm test` and confirm the auth tests pass reliably in CI.
docs/keycloak/ planned: document Keycloak setup, roles, tenant claim, and env vars.
Auth error cases return deterministic 401/403 responses and never leak tokens in logs.
docs/guides/user-management/ planned: add a non-technical login and roles overview guide.
subtasks.output: Auth tests passing and documentation added

changelog: Added Keycloak OIDC authentication for the dashboard using server-side sessions
